title: 10 best herbal teas for stomach cramps
intro_desc: Herbal teas for stomach cramps are a natural remedy that offers relief from abdominal pain, bloating, and discomfort caused by digestive issues. These teas work by soothing the gut lining, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the muscles in the stomach. Examples of herbal teas that help with stomach cramps include peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing instant relief from discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy their daily activities without interruption or pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with stomach cramps because its active compound, menthol, relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. This relaxation reduces muscle spasms and inflammation that can cause cramping. Additionally, peppermint's natural antibacterial properties help to alleviate gut infections that may be contributing to the discomfort. As the tea is absorbed into the body, it soothes the digestive system, providing quick relief from stomach cramps and promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce spasms, relieve pain, and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, providing quick relief from stomach cramps.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint tea, such as menthone and limonene, have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to soothe irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms, reduce inflammation, and ease stomach cramps.

Antispasmodic Compounds: Peppermint tea contains antispasmodic compounds that help to relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms and relieving symptoms of stomach cramps, indigestion, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in relieving stomach cramps and spasms.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain menthol and other compounds that help to soothe irritated mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning after dew has dried for maximum flavor and aroma.
Rinse 1 tablespoon of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint with cold filtered water.
Bring 8 ounces of filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat within 5-7 minutes.
Add the rinsed peppermint leaves to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes to release natural oils and flavors.
Strain tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids after 2-3 minutes of settling time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with stomach cramps because its natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe the digestive system, reducing irritation and spasms. The tea's apigenin and flavonoids calm the muscles in the intestines, easing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's gentle sedative effects can help relax the body and reduce stress, a common trigger for stomach cramps. By promoting relaxation and calming digestive issues, German chamomile teas provide natural relief from stomach cramps, allowing for improved digestion and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German chamomile tea contains flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing muscle cramps and spasms that can cause pain and discomfort.

Soothing mucilages: German chamomile tea contains mucilages, such as apigenin-7-glucoside and luteolin-4'-glucoside, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the gut, making them effective in alleviating stomach cramps.
Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps relax the muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla plants or purchase high-quality herbal tea blends.
Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat water in a tea kettle or pot and bring it to a boil then let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Pour the hot brewed tea into a teapot or cups and let it cool down slightly to prevent scorching.
Strain the tea leaves from the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and serve hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with stomach cramps because its calming properties help to soothe the digestive system. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the abdominal area, alleviating cramp-like discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm's gentle relaxant effects can help regulate digestive rhythm, easing spasms and tension that often contribute to stomach cramps. As a result, sipping on a warm cup of herbal lemon balm tea may provide instant relief from cramping and promote a sense of calm in the digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with stomach cramps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to stomach cramp pain and discomfort.
Calming effects on the nervous system: The tea's soothing effects on the nervous system help to calm digestive spasms and reduce anxiety, which can exacerbate stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to soothe and calm digestive issues such as stomach cramps.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat stomach cramps due to their calming properties and ability to reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves at peak flavor and aroma for best results.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves per 8 oz cup.
Heat filtered water to a simmer in a tea kettle or pot over low to moderate heat.
Steep the lemon balm leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to release optimal flavor and essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve immediately or store in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with stomach cramps because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly catechins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The gentle, subtle flavor of white tea also makes it easy to digest, allowing for a smooth and uninterrupted flow of nutrients into the body. Additionally, white tea's natural caffeine content can help to stimulate digestion and relieve bloating and discomfort associated with cramps.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Herbal white teas like peppermint and chamomile contain compounds that can help to dry up excess moisture in the digestive tract, which can provide relief from stomach cramps caused by diarrhea or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like ginger and fennel in herbal white tea blends have natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which may contribute to stomach cramps and discomfort.

Sooothing compounds: Herbal white teas like lemon balm and licorice root contain soothing compounds like flavonoids and saponins that can help to calm the digestive system and provide relief from stomach cramps, nausea, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to soothe and calm digestive discomfort.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and ease stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of high-quality white tea leaves per eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 150 to 160 degrees fahrenheit for five minutes.
Steep the tea leaves in hot water for two to three minutes or according to package instructions.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or fine-mesh sieve and discard solids.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool to your desired temperature within five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with stomach cramps because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, it can help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause stomach cramps. Additionally, licorice root has natural antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, further alleviating cramp-like symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal licorice teas can provide relief from stomach cramps and promote digestive comfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach cramps.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in licorice tea may help to relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, relieving spasms and cramps that can cause abdominal pain.

Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help reduce inflammation and ease muscle contractions in the digestive system, providing relief from stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing stomach cramps.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to calm digestive issues and reduce pain from stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots from your garden or purchase them from a reputable source.
Rinse the roots thoroughly with cold water and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 10 grams.
Combine the chopped roots with one liter of boiling distilled water in a large pot and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool completely before transferring it to glass bottles and storing it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with stomach cramps because of its unique ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract. The tea's active compounds, including anethole and fenchone, work together to soothe spasms and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from cramping pain. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, which can contribute to stomach discomfort. As a natural remedy, fennel tea has been used for centuries to calm digestive issues, making it an effective and comforting solution for those suffering from stomach cramps.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tea's carminative properties help to alleviate digestive discomfort, bloating, and cramping by reducing gas and relieving pressure in the intestines.
Spasmolytic properties: Fennel tea's spasmolytic properties help to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can lead to abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach cramps and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and antioxidants, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Contain a compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties and can help relieve spasms.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried or fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured fennel leaves with the boiling water in a large heat-resistant glass measuring cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herb's active compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove any solids.
Store the cooled fennel tea in an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway teas helps with stomach cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and relax the digestive tract. The caraway seeds contain a compound called carvone, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease spasms in the muscles of the stomach and intestines. This can provide relief from crampy discomfort and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion. Additionally, the calming effects of caraway tea can help regulate bowel movements and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Carvone: Carvone, a compound found in caraway seeds, has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with stomach cramps.

Cholinergic activity: The cholinergic compounds present in caraway tea can stimulate the release of digestive enzymes, relax the muscles in the digestive tract, and improve gut motility, which helps to alleviate cramping and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: Caraway tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the smooth muscle linings of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), dyspepsia, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, caraway roots have been traditionally used to alleviate digestive issues and soothe stomach cramps.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a compound called cumic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi leaves and seeds or use dried ones of equal quality.
Combine one teaspoon of crushed caraway seeds with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane teas helps with stomach cramps because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated digestive linings, reducing discomfort and spasms. The tea's flavonoids also enhance digestion by stimulating the release of stomach enzymes, breaking down food more efficiently. Additionally, elecampane's natural antispasmodic properties relax muscle contractions in the stomach, calming cramping and discomfort associated with indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome. By addressing these underlying issues, elecampane teas provide relief from stomach cramps, promoting a sense of calm and digestive balance.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Elecampane's sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach cramps caused by inflammatory conditions.

Antispasmodic effects: The triterpenoid saponins present in elecampane tea exhibit antispasmodic activity, which helps relax smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from stomach cramps and discomfort.

Digestive soothing properties: Elecampane's mucilages, such as hemicellulose and pectin, help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elecampane roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them effective in treating stomach cramps.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the elecampane plant contains sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate stomach cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried elecampane roots and leaves from trusted sources.
Dry the harvested material thoroughly in a warm place for several hours.
Combine one tablespoon of dried elecampane root with boiling water for brewing.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea and discard solids before serving hot or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with stomach cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract. The bioactive compounds found in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause smooth muscle contractions, leading to abdominal cramps. By relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation, ginger teas help to alleviate stomach cramps and provide relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzymes: Ginger's digestive enzymes help to break down food more efficiently, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea associated with stomach cramps.

Inflammation Reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by stomach cramps.
Spasm Relief: Ginger's natural relaxant properties help to ease muscle spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from abdominal cramping and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help soothe digestive issues and relieve stomach cramps.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant is a primary source of its medicinal properties, providing anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects for stomach cramp relief.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one inch piece of fresh ginger root under cold running water gently scrubbing off any dirt.
Peel the ginger root using a vegetable peeler removing the skin in long strips.
Grate two teaspoons of peeled ginger root into a large pot using a box grater or fine cheese grater.
Pour four cups of boiling water over the grated ginger allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena teas helps with stomach cramps because of its soothing and calming properties. The tea's active compounds, such as citronellol and linalool, work to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, verbena tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to cramping. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, verbena teas provide relief from stomach cramps, helping to alleviate discomfort and improve overall digestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Verbena tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants present in verbena tea help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to stomach cramps and discomfort caused by conditions such as gastritis or enteritis.

Antioxidant properties: Verbena tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage gut lining and exacerbate stomach cramps, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Verbena roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing stomach cramps caused by inflammation.
Leaves: Fresh verbena leaves contain essential oils that help to relax the digestive tract muscles, reducing spasms and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena leaves and flowers from well-maintained gardens or purchase dried herb from reputable suppliers.
Measure one teaspoon of dried verbena leaves and flowers or two teaspoons of fresh herb for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup to allow the ingredients to steep for ten minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove the solids and discard the remaining herbs.
Serve the cooled verbena tea hot or chilled, sweetened with honey or sugar if desired, and enjoy within two hours of preparation time.
